title: asclepias circinalis
intro: Asclepias circinalis, also known as the Antelope Horn Milkweed, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a member of the Asclepias genus, characterized by its unique, horn-shaped seed pods and white to purple flowers. Asclepias circinalis has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which make it beneficial for treating various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or ingested under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
